title: Iteration
description: What loops are, the three loop types in Scratch, and four hands-on builds: repeat loop, forever bounce, key-controlled movement, and a catch game.

steps.title: Do Now Activity
steps.content:
Do Now

Which block makes a sprite run the same code indefinitely?
steps.js: createMCQ('iter-dna',['repeat (10)','forever','repeat until','if/else'],1,'Correct — the forever loop runs without stopping.','The forever loop keeps repeating its contents indefinitely.');

steps.title: Loop Types
steps.content:
The three loop types

Iteration means repeating instructions using a loop — much cleaner than writing the same blocks over and over.

repeat (5)
  move (10) steps
end
— runs exactly 5 times
forever
  move (5) steps
  if on edge, bounce
end
— runs until the program stops
repeat until <touching [edge v]?>
  move (5) steps
end
— runs until the sprite hits the edge

Choose repeat when you know a fixed count, forever for continuous game loops, and repeat until when you are waiting for something to happen.

steps.title: Build 1: Repeat Loop
steps.content:
Build 1: repeat loop — draw a square

Use a repeat (4) loop to draw a square. Each side needs a move and a turn.

when green flag clicked
pen down
repeat (4)
  move (100) steps
  turn right (90) degrees
end
pen up

The pen blocks are in the Pen extension — click the blue + button at the bottom-left of the block palette to add it.

  1. Add the Pen extension (blue + → Pen)
  2. Build the script above
  3. Experiment: change 4 to 3 for a triangle, or 6 for a hexagon

w=f.parentElement,bar=w.querySelector('.y7-check-bar'),bH=bar?bar.offsetHeight:44,fs=w.classList.contains('y7-fullscreen'),aW=fs?window.innerWidth:w.clientWidth,sc=fs?Math.min(aW/NW,(window.innerHeight-bH)/NH):aW/NW;f.style.width=NW+'px';f.style.height=NH+'px';f.style.transform='scale('+sc+')';f.style.marginBottom=Math.round(NH*(sc-1))+'px';f.style.marginLeft=fs?Math.max(0,Math.round((aW-NW*sc)/2))+'px':'0';}function f=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame'),w=f.parentElement,b=document.getElementById('y7-fs-btn'),fs=w.classList.toggle('y7-fullscreen');b.textContent=fs?'✕':'⛶';if(fs){window._sy=window.scrollY;document.body.style.overflow='hidden';}else{document.body.style.overflow='';window.scrollTo(0,window._sy||0);}sf();}sf();if(window._sfR)window.removeEventListener('resize',window._sfR);window._sfR=sf;window.addEventListener('resize',sf);if(window._sfK)document.removeEventListener('keydown',window._sfK);window._sfK=function(e){if(e.key==='Escape'){var f=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame');if(f&&f.parentElement.classList.contains('y7-fullscreen'))tfs();}};document.addEventListener('keydown',window._sfK);document.getElementById('y7-fs-btn').onclick=tfs;document.getElementById('y7-check-btn').onclick=function(){var f=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame'),r=document.getElementById('y7-check-result');try{var vm=f.contentWindow.vm;if(!vm){r.style.color='#fca5a5';r.textContent='Editor still loading.';return;}var T=vm.runtime.targets,hRep=false,hMove=false,hTurn=false;for(var t=0;t<T.length;t++){var B=T[t].blocks._blocks;for(var id in B){var b=B[id];if(b.opcode==='control_repeat')hRep=true;if(b.opcode==='motion_movesteps')hMove=true;if(b.opcode==='motion_turnright'||b.opcode==='motion_turnleft')hTurn=true;}}if(hRep&&hMove&&hTurn){r.style.color='#86efac';r.textContent='✓ Repeat loop with move and turn found!';__markStepComplete();}else{var m=[];if(!hRep)m.push('repeat loop');if(!hMove)m.push('move steps');if(!hTurn)m.push('turn');r.style.color='#fca5a5';r.textContent='Missing: '+m.join(', ');}}catch(e){r.style.color='#fca5a5';r.textContent='Error: '+e.message;}};})()

steps.title: Build 2: Arrow Key Controls
steps.content:
Build 2: arrow key controls

This is the standard pattern for player-controlled movement. A forever loop keeps checking the keys every frame.

when green flag clicked
forever
  if <key [right arrow v] pressed?> then
    change x by (5)
  end
  if <key [left arrow v] pressed?> then
    change x by (-5)
  end
  if <key [up arrow v] pressed?> then
    change y by (5)
  end
  if <key [down arrow v] pressed?> then
    change y by (-5)
  end
end
  1. Build the script above — four if blocks inside a forever loop
  2. The key pressed? block is in Sensing (light blue)
  3. Run it and move with the arrow keys

f=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame');if(f&&f.parentElement.classList.contains('y7-fullscreen'))tfs();}};document.addEventListener('keydown',window._sfK);document.getElementById('y7-fs-btn').onclick=tfs;document.getElementById('y7-check-btn').onclick=function(){var f=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame'),r=document.getElementById('y7-check-result');try{var vm=f.contentWindow.vm;if(!vm){r.style.color='#fca5a5';r.textContent='Editor still loading.';return;}var T=vm.runtime.targets,keys=[];for(var t=0;t<T.length;t++){var B=T[t].blocks._blocks;for(var id in B){var b=B[id];if(b.opcode==='sensing_keypressed'&&b.fields&&b.fields.KEY_OPTION)keys.push(b.fields.KEY_OPTION[0]);}}var hR=keys.indexOf('right arrow')!==-1,hL=keys.indexOf('left arrow')!==-1,hU=keys.indexOf('up arrow')!==-1,hD=keys.indexOf('down arrow')!==-1;if(hR&&hL){r.style.color='#86efac';r.textContent='✓ Arrow key controls detected!'+(hU&&hD?' All four directions!':' Add up and down too!');if(hR&&hL&&hU&&hD)__markStepComplete();}else{var m=[];if(!hR)m.push('right arrow');if(!hL)m.push('left arrow');if(!hU)m.push('up arrow');if(!hD)m.push('down arrow');r.style.color='#fca5a5';r.textContent='Missing: '+m.join(', ');}}catch(e){r.style.color='#fca5a5';r.textContent='Error: '+e.message;}};})()

steps.title: Build 3: Catch Game
steps.content:
Build 3: catch game

Move the catcher left and right to catch the falling ball. Score a point each catch.

Sprite 1 — Catcher (player)

when green flag clicked
set [score v] to (0)
forever
  if <key [right arrow v] pressed?> then
    change x by (10)
  end
  if <key [left arrow v] pressed?> then
    change x by (-10)
  end
end

Sprite 2 — Ball (add a second sprite)

when green flag clicked
go to x: (0) y: (180)
forever
  change y by (-5)
  if <touching [Sprite1 v]?> then
    go to x: (pick random (-200) to (200)) y: (180)
    change [score v] by (1)
  end
  if <(y position) < (-170)> then
    go to x: (pick random (-200) to (200)) y: (180)
  end
end

f=document.getElementById('y7-scratch-frame'),r=document.getElementById('y7-check-result');try{var vm=f.contentWindow.vm;if(!vm){r.style.color='#fca5a5';r.textContent='Editor still loading.';return;}var T=vm.runtime.targets,hForever=false,hKeys=false,hChangeY=false,hIf=false,hScore=false;for(var t=0;t<T.length;t++){var B=T[t].blocks._blocks;for(var id in B){var b=B[id];if(b.opcode==='control_forever')hForever=true;if(b.opcode==='sensing_keypressed')hKeys=true;if(b.opcode==='motion_changeyby')hChangeY=true;if(b.opcode==='control_if'||b.opcode==='control_if_else')hIf=true;if(b.opcode==='data_changevariableby')hScore=true;}}var spriteCount=T.filter(function(t){return !t.isStage;}).length;if(hForever&&hKeys&&hChangeY&&hIf&&spriteCount>=2){r.style.color='#86efac';r.textContent='✓ Catch game complete!'+(hScore?' Score variable found!':' Tip: add a score variable.');__markStepComplete();}else{var m=[];if(!hForever)m.push('forever loop');if(!hKeys)m.push('key sensing');if(!hChangeY)m.push('change y (falling)');if(!hIf)m.push('if block');if(spriteCount<2)m.push('2 sprites');r.style.color='#fca5a5';r.textContent='Missing: '+m.join(', ');}}catch(e){r.style.color='#fca5a5';r.textContent='Error: '+e.message;}};})()

quizQuestions.q: Which loop runs forever without stopping?
repeat (6) loop runs 'change score by 2'. Score starts at 0. What is score at the end?
Which loop stops when a condition becomes true?
Which script correctly makes a sprite move right when the right arrow is held?
options: when flag clicked → forever → if key right pressed → change x by 5
when right arrow pressed → move steps

quizQuestions.q: Build a script using a repeat (4) loop that moves the sprite and turns it after each move. Click Check & Submit.
quizQuestions.sampleAnswer: when flag clicked → repeat (4) → move 100 steps → turn right 90 degrees

quizQuestions.q: Build a script that uses a forever loop to move the sprite and bounce off the edge. Click Check & Submit.
quizQuestions.sampleAnswer: when flag clicked → forever → move 5 steps → if on edge, bounce

quizQuestions.q: Build a sprite that moves right when right arrow is pressed and left when left arrow is pressed (using a forever loop). Click Check & Submit.
quizQuestions.sampleAnswer: forever → if right arrow pressed → change x 5 → if left arrow pressed → change x -5
